content: Self-Refine's task-agnostic iterative self-feedback loop can be modularly augmented with Self-Contrast's multiple inconsistent solving perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms to resolve a key hidden limitation of vanilla Self-Refine: the tendency to get stuck in local optima caused by the model's consistent blind spots in single-perspective feedback. This hybrid framework retains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no extra training or labeled data, while leveraging multi-perspective reflection to surface unrecognized flaws in initial outputs that single-perspective self-feedback would miss.
